# TheDomeBros

> TheDomeBros LLC sells and installs air-supported pool domes (inflatable vinyl pool enclosures) in Utah County, Utah — Provo, Orem, Lehi, American Fork, Spanish Fork, Pleasant Grove, Saratoga Springs, Eagle Mountain, Highland, Alpine, Cedar Hills, Mapleton, Springville, and Payson. A dome turns an existing outdoor pool into a year-round swimmable space: a quiet blower keeps the vinyl dome inflated (no poles or framing), it anchors to the pool deck, goes up for the cold months, and comes down and stores for summer.

Key facts:
- A pool dome is a seasonal, air-supported pool enclosure — a fraction of the cost of a permanent glass or framed enclosure, with no foundations and usually no permit (treated as a temporary structure / pool cover in most jurisdictions; customers verify locally).
- It traps radiant heat from the water and stops evaporation, typically cutting pool heating costs roughly in half; the blower cycles on a pressure switch, so its electricity cost is modest.
- Handles snow (melts off or the dome settles onto the warm water) and typical windstorms (owners report riding out 60 mph gusts); usable to about −10°F. Cold climates are ideal — domes last ~9–12 years there.
- TheDomeBros handles measurement, custom sizing, installation, seasonal takedown, and storage locally.
- Contact: contact@thedomebros.com · (385) 204-4760 · free quotes.
